note: Non-NIH (society & foundation) funding opportunities that commonly support hematology-oncology fellows and early-career faculty. Amounts and deadlines change yearly and many run on recurring cycles — always confirm on the official page before relying on a date. To add an opportunity, append an entry below.

opportunities.name: ASH Research Training Award for Fellows (RTAF)
opportunities.duration: 1 year (up to a 2nd year for some)
opportunities.eligibility: MD or MD-PhD, 0–4 years from residency, in a hematology / heme-onc training program.

deadlines.label: Full application
deadlines.note: 2026 cycle closed (was ~mid-January). Next cycle typically due mid-January — verify on the ASH portal.

opportunities.typical_timing: Applications typically due mid-January for funding that summer.

opportunities.name: ASH Scholar Award
opportunities.eligibility: Fellows in mentored postdoctoral positions, or junior faculty (Assistant Professor or equivalent).
opportunities.typical_timing: LOI early May; invited full application early August.

opportunities.name: Conquer Cancer Young Investigator Award (YIA)
opportunities.focus: Clinical oncology (all tumor types; many subspecialty competitions)
opportunities.eligibility: Physicians transitioning from fellowship to a faculty appointment; conducted under a mentor.

deadlines.label: Varies by competition
deadlines.note: Multiple competitions with different deadlines (general + subspecialty/special, e.g. Drug Development, Survivorship, Pediatric Brain Tumors, AACR-Conquer Cancer Translational). Check the ASCO YIA page for the open competition.

opportunities.typical_timing: General and special competitions open through the year; many deadlines fall winter–summer.

opportunities.name: Conquer Cancer Career Development Award (CDA)
opportunities.eligibility: Clinical investigators who have received their initial faculty appointment and are building an independent clinical cancer research program.

deadlines.label: General competition
deadlines.note: Confirm the current cycle deadline on the ASCO CDA page.

opportunities.typical_timing: Annual cycle; see the ASCO Career Development Award page.

opportunities.name: LLS Career Development Program (CDP)
opportunities.org: Leukemia & Lymphoma Society / Blood Cancer United
opportunities.eligibility: Tiered by stage (Fellow, Special Fellow, Scholar/Scholar in Clinical Research). Childcare stipend up to $3,600/yr.

deadlines.label: Application
deadlines.note: 2025/26 cycle was ~Oct 17. Next cycle typically due mid-October — verify in the Blood Cancer United / LLS portal.

opportunities.name: Damon Runyon Physician-Scientist Training Award
opportunities.amount: $100k → $130k/yr ($460k total) + up to $100k med-school debt retirement
opportunities.eligibility: MD/MD-PhD physician-scientists pursuing mentored cancer research.

opportunities.name: ACS Postdoctoral Fellowship
opportunities.eligibility: Postdoctoral / clinical fellows early in mentored research training.
opportunities.typical_timing: Two cycles a year: June 1 and December 1.

opportunities.name: ACS Clinician Scientist Development Grant (CSDG)
opportunities.eligibility: Full-time faculty with clinical responsibilities who do not yet lead an independent research program.
opportunities.typical_timing: Two cycles a year: June 1 and December 1.

opportunities.name: AACR Research Fellowships
opportunities.focus: Basic / translational / clinical cancer research (themed fellowships)
opportunities.eligibility: Mentored early-career fellows; many themed tracks (e.g., Basic, Immuno-oncology, Cancer Disparities, disease-specific).

deadlines.label: Varies by fellowship
deadlines.note: Each themed fellowship has its own deadline (e.g., 2026 Barker Basic ~Mar 3, Cancer Disparities ~Mar 12, FAACR Academy Impact ~Jun 30). See the AACR funding page for open opportunities.

opportunities.typical_timing: Most deadlines fall late winter through early summer.

opportunities.name: Lymphoma Research Foundation Postdoctoral Fellowship
opportunities.eligibility: PhD fellows <5 yrs postdoc, or MD fellows 2–5 yrs into fellowship, in lymphoma research.

deadlines.label: Application
deadlines.note: Confirm the current cycle on the LRF grants page.

opportunities.typical_timing: Annual cycle; RFP usually posted in spring.

opportunities.name: Prostate Cancer Foundation Young Investigator Award
opportunities.eligibility: Early-career physicians/scientists in prostate cancer research with a mentor.

deadlines.label: Application
deadlines.note: Review in spring, decisions by early summer — confirm on the PCF open RFAs page.

opportunities.typical_timing: RFA opens late in the year; review in spring.

opportunities.name: Burroughs Wellcome Career Award for Medical Scientists (CAMS)
opportunities.eligibility: MD/MD-PhD physician-scientists bridging advanced training to early faculty. Institutional nomination required.

deadlines.label: Nomination
deadlines.note: Institutional (limited-submission) nomination, typically early October — check with your research office and the BWF page.

opportunities.typical_timing: Annual; nomination window usually early autumn.

opportunities.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative (CZI) Science
opportunities.focus: Biomedical science (programs rotate; some cancer/immunology relevant)
opportunities.eligibility: Program-specific. CZI runs rotating RFAs and resource programs rather than a standing fellowship; relevance to heme/onc varies by cycle.

deadlines.label: Rotating RFAs
deadlines.note: No standing fellowship — watch CZI's open opportunities for relevant calls.

opportunities.typical_timing: Opportunities open on rolling/rotating RFAs throughout the year.
